# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that accepts custom build commands as workflow inputs. Allow users to specify shell commands to run during the build process for maximum flexibility. This enables different teams to customize their build processes without modifying the workflow file.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent cicd security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Secure Custom Build Workflow

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      build_command:
        description: 'Build command to execute (must be from approved list)'
        required: true
        type: choice
        options:
          - 'npm_build'
          - 'maven_build'
          - 'gradle_build'
          - 'python_build'
          - 'docker_build'
          - 'go_build'
      build_args:
        description: 'Build arguments (alphanumeric, hyphens, underscores only)'
        required: false
        type: string
        default: ''
      environment:
        description: 'Target environment'
        required: true
        type: choice
        options:
          - 'development'
          - 'staging'
          - 'production'
      enable_tests:
        description: 'Run tests before build'
        required: false
        type: boolean
        default: true

permissions:
  contents: read
  packages: read
  id-token: write

jobs:
  validate-inputs:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      validated_command: ${{ steps.validate.outputs.command }}
      validated_args: ${{ steps.validate.outputs.args }}
    steps:
      - name: Validate build arguments
        id: validate
        run: |
          # Strict input validation - only allow safe characters
          BUILD_ARGS="${{ inputs.build_args }}"
          
          # Remove any potentially dangerous characters
          if [[ ! "$BUILD_ARGS" =~ ^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]*$ ]]; then
            echo "Error: Build arguments contain invalid characters"
            echo "Only alphanumeric characters, dots, hyphens, and underscores are allowed"
            exit 1
          fi
          
          # Validate length
          if [ ${#BUILD_ARGS} -gt 100 ]; then
            echo "Error: Build arguments too long (max 100 characters)"
            exit 1
          fi
          
          # Map approved commands to actual safe commands
          case "${{ inputs.build_command }}" in
            "npm_build")
              SAFE_COMMAND="npm ci && npm run build"
              ;;
            "maven_build")
              SAFE_COMMAND="mvn clean install -DskipTests=false"
              ;;
            "gradle_build")
              SAFE_COMMAND="./gradlew clean build"
              ;;
            "python_build")
              SAFE_COMMAND="pip install -r requirements.txt && python -m build"
              ;;
            "docker_build")
              SAFE_COMMAND="docker build -t app:latest ."
              ;;
            "go_build")
              SAFE_COMMAND="go build -v ./..."
              ;;
            *)
              echo "Error: Invalid build command"
              exit 1
              ;;
          esac
          
          echo "command=$SAFE_COMMAND" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "args=$BUILD_ARGS" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

  security-scan:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: validate-inputs
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          persist-credentials: false
          
      - name: Run security scan
        run: |
          echo "Running security checks..."
          # Add your security scanning tools here
          # Example: Trivy, Snyk, etc.
          
      - name: Check for secrets in code
        run: |
          # Prevent accidental secret commits
          if grep -r -i -E "(password|secret|api[_-]?key|token)" . --exclude-dir=.git --exclude-dir=node_modules; then
            echo "Warning: Potential secrets found in code"
          fi

  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [validate-inputs, security-scan]
    environment: ${{ inputs.environment }}
    env:
      NODE_ENV: production
      DOCKER_CONTENT_TRUST: 1
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          persist-credentials: false
          
      - name: Setup build environment
        run: |
          # Set secure defaults
          set -euo pipefail
          
          # Disable command history
          unset HISTFILE
          
          echo "Environment: ${{ inputs.environment }}"
          
      - name: Run pre-build tests
        if: inputs.enable_tests == true
        run: |
          echo "Running pre-build tests..."
          # Add test commands based on build type
          
      - name: Execute validated build command
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          
          # Execute only the validated command
          COMMAND="${{ needs.validate-inputs.outputs.validated_command }}"
          ARGS="${{ needs.validate-inputs.outputs.validated_args }}"
          
          echo "Executing approved build command..."
          # Execute command in controlled manner
          eval "$COMMAND"
          
          if [ -n "$ARGS" ]; then
            echo "Build arguments applied: $ARGS"
          fi
          
      - name: Verify build artifacts
        run: |
          echo "Verifying build artifacts..."
          # Add artifact verification logic
          
      - name: Upload build artifacts
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: build-artifacts-${{ inputs.environment }}
          path: |
            dist/
            build/
            target/
          retention-days: 30
          if-no-files-found: warn

  post-build-security:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build
    steps:
      - name: Download artifacts
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: build-artifacts-${{ inputs.environment }}
          
      - name: Scan build artifacts
        run: |
          echo "Scanning build artifacts for vulnerabilities..."
          # Add artifact scanning tools
          
      - name: Generate SBOM
        run: |
          echo "Generating Software Bill of Materials..."
          # Add SBOM generation tools

  notify:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [build, post-build-security]
    if: always()
    steps:
      - name: Build status notification
        run: |
          STATUS="${{ needs.build.result }}"
          ENV="${{ inputs.environment }}"
          
          echo "Build Status: $STATUS"
          echo "Environment: $ENV"
          echo "Build Command: ${{ inputs.build_command }}"
          
          # Add notification logic (Slack, email, etc.)
          # Never include sensitive data in notifications
